message:
feat(workflows/runner): tick 180 run_workflow_inner shared impl threads cx through 4 startup storage sites (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Third tick of the workflows/runner.rs strangler-fig. Ships the
first actual cx-threading into the workflow startup handshake.

Refactor: introduced `run_workflow_inner(cx: Option<&Cx>, ...)` as
the shared implementation. Both public methods delegate:
- `run_workflow` → `run_workflow_inner(None, ...)`
- `run_workflow_with_cx` → (after pre-flight checkpoint)
  `run_workflow_inner(Some(cx), ...)`

When cx is `Some`, 4 startup storage call sites route through
their cx-first siblings:
- `record_workflow_start_action` → `_with_cx` (tick 179;
  internally composite of record_audit_action_redacted_with_cx +
  upsert_action_undo_redacted_with_cx)
- `fetch_workflow_start_action_id` → `_with_cx` (tick 179;
  routes through get_audit_actions_with_cx)
- `storage.get_workflow` → `get_workflow_with_cx` (existing)
- `storage.get_pane` → `get_pane_with_cx` (existing)

When cx is `None` (legacy caller), behavior is byte-for-byte
identical to the pre-tick-180 body — each branch has an explicit
ambient-path arm gated by `if let Some(cx) = cx`.

Step-loop portion (after ctx setup) does not yet thread cx —
per the strangler-fig plan from tick 177, per-step checkpoints
and per-step-branch storage calls migrate in tick 181+.

No public API changes. Legacy `run_workflow` signature unchanged.
`run_workflow_with_cx` signature unchanged (already cx-taking
from tick 178).

Verified via fork bypass: 31/31 workflows::runner:: tests pass.
No regression. The cx-threaded startup is dead cancellation-wise
(nothing currently invokes `run_workflow_with_cx` with a
cancellable cx) but the infrastructure is in place.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
